[2018-04-05 21:07] gadget246: does vore always beat amp or am i stupid
[2018-04-05 21:09] crash_overlord: Pretty much, outside of fuge (potentialy)
[2018-04-05 21:09] crash_overlord: Anything fast beats ampo
[2018-04-05 21:12] darkeisbein: maybe if there is a crazy ammount of good defense in the set?
[2018-04-05 21:13] gadget246: how does p2 open here?
ck1qY-p@cHc
[2018-04-05 21:13] gadget246: because with my opening p1 is always half a turn ahead and wins because of it
[2018-04-05 21:13] gadget246: and i'm not sure how else i could have opened
[2018-04-05 21:14] .bky_1556 (bky): DD and fork depending on whether they got the third engineer.
[2018-04-05 21:14] Deleted User (Deleted User): amp is never proper without support
[2018-04-05 21:14] .bky_1556 (bky): "amp is never proper without support" -> unless your opponent did something horribly wrong.
[2018-04-05 21:15] darkeisbein: yeah amp punishes durdle
[2018-04-05 21:15] gadget246: ok, thanks for the help with amp
new question is the opener for that set
[2018-04-05 21:17] gadget246: i'm not sure how to get my shadowfang online before my opponent as p2
[2018-04-05 21:17] Deleted User (Deleted User): still, without support it's generally safer to do another punish
[2018-04-05 21:25] mrguy888: @Steel you are either misremembering the context or the person who said that.
[2018-04-05 21:26] mrguy888: The closest I can think of is a set with vore and 7 other units where I said I didn't like elyot animus.
[2018-04-05 21:26] .bky_1556 (bky): You can get beat fang with tarsiers, walls and cryo rays I think.
[2018-04-05 21:27] .bky_1556 (bky): Don't know whether Wincer rush is fast enough off the top of my head; I'd need to analyze.
[2018-04-05 21:27] p0lari (Polari): I would have gone wincer without thinking too hard about it
[2018-04-05 21:32] p0lari (Polari): 2.dd doesn't make sense though
[2018-04-05 21:32] p0lari (Polari): for any decent plan in that set you'd want either dde or ddc
[2018-04-05 21:40] gadget246: what makes third engineer or natural conduit the right opener there?
[2018-04-05 21:42] p0lari (Polari): you're going to want either odin or wincer
[2018-04-05 21:42] f300xen (f0x): natural conduit is always a good opener, unless it isn't
[2018-04-05 21:42] p0lari (Polari): one of them is probably just better than the other but I don't know which one
[2018-04-05 21:43] p0lari (Polari): I tried both against a dumb dd dd aa 1 1t 1 1t 1... p1 line and both dd dde into odin and dd ddc into wincer won easily
[2018-04-05 21:43] gadget246: what part of this is not just memorizing openings?
[2018-04-05 21:44] gadget246: or is it just straight up memorizing openings
[2018-04-05 21:44] p0lari (Polari): actually for the first one the t2 engi didn't even matter because I just did 3.ddb, so you might as well just have skipped it and got 3.ddeb
[2018-04-05 21:45] p0lari (Polari): you get third engi if you want high econ early (which you'll want for odin) and natural conduit is just efficient
[2018-04-05 21:45] p0lari (Polari): you don't need memorisation to realise that floating 4 gold on turn 2 is pretty bad in a game of exponential growth
[2018-04-05 21:46] gadget246: so basically my opener was wrong
I don't understand why
and you can't tell me why
or why your openers are better
they just are
[2018-04-05 21:46] p0lari (Polari): just like in rts games your units are investments that produce constant returns
[2018-04-05 21:47] p0lari (Polari): at its most simple buying a drone for 3 gold now gives you a return of 1 gold every turn for the rest of the game
[2018-04-05 21:47] p0lari (Polari): for other units it's not quite as straightforward but the same principle applies
[2018-04-05 21:47] gadget246: yeah i understand inflation
[2018-04-05 21:48] p0lari (Polari): ok so basically there are two things going on here
[2018-04-05 21:48] gadget246: but how does getting my shadowfang even later help with the problem of my shadowfang being half a turn slower?
or does p2 just not have the option to go for shadowfang
[2018-04-05 21:48] p0lari (Polari): one is that random set units are generally more powerful than base set units, and some random set units are more impactful than others
[2018-04-05 21:48] p0lari (Polari): and you want to craft your build around getting the best units in the pool
[2018-04-05 21:49] p0lari (Polari): which in this case is odin or wincer
[2018-04-05 21:49] mqp: i guarantee neither player should be getting shadowfang with correct play in that set
[2018-04-05 21:49] p0lari (Polari): the other thing is that whichever plan you're going for you want to spend your resources efficiently each turn
[2018-04-05 21:49] gadget246: in which case my problem is not memorizing openers but memorizing which units are stronger
[2018-04-05 21:49] gadget246: unless someone can explain to me what makes shadowfang bad
[2018-04-05 21:50] mqp: sure, it's easy to explain
[2018-04-05 21:50] f300xen (f0x): shadowfang is bad because second animus is expensive
[2018-04-05 21:50] mqp: when you get AA for shadowfang the only thing that second animus is useful for is going to be shadowfang
[2018-04-05 21:50] mqp: if you don't buy at least 3 or 4 shadowfangs, it won't have been a good investment
[2018-04-05 21:50] mqp: in this case odin and the wincer threaten a ton of damage quickly (edit: and cryo ray)

[2018-04-05 21:50] p0lari (Polari): I don't understand why you're so confrontational about this
[2018-04-05 21:51] mqp: if your opponent gets those units you will have to defend for a large number
[2018-04-05 21:51] mqp: you won't be able to simultaneously defend for a large number soon enough and get shadowfangs
[2018-04-05 21:51] mqp: so the second animus will not pay off
[2018-04-05 21:51] p0lari (Polari): I'm not sure if we can concisely explain to you right here, but I can assure you that with a solid understanding of the game's fundamentals it's really not hard to see shadowfang isn't going to work out there
[2018-04-05 21:51] gadget246: how do i gain that understanding
[2018-04-05 21:52] p0lari (Polari): read guides, play, analyse your play
[2018-04-05 21:53] gadget246: that's what i'm doing
so far the only things i'm gaining are memorizations of what units are strong and weak
[2018-04-05 21:53] p0lari (Polari): sounds like your expectations are just set way too high
[2018-04-05 21:53] p0lari (Polari): nobody got good at the game in a day
[2018-04-05 21:54] p0lari (Polari): I mean memorisation *is* always an issue for a game with no randomness or hidden information and I've been wary of that since I started playing
[2018-04-05 21:54] p0lari (Polari): but the game was fun enough that I've stuck with it anyway
[2018-04-05 21:55] p0lari (Polari): and I can also tell you that rote memorisation hasn't been a thing along the way
[2018-04-05 21:56] gadget246: the problem i have is that i'm not gaining understanding of anything
i'm not learning
i'm just memorizing things like 'fang is worse than odin wincer'
or 'lucina wins games if you can afford her'
[2018-04-05 21:57] mqp: then stop memorizing them. those facts are all derivable from other important knowledge
[2018-04-05 21:57] p0lari (Polari): fang isn't just universally worse than those
[2018-04-05 21:57] gadget246: how do i gain that knowledge mquander
[2018-04-05 21:57] p0lari (Polari): but in a small set where you're going to have to pick one and play it with barely any support it's not going to win
[2018-04-05 21:57] p0lari (Polari): for reasons mquander just tried to explain to you
[2018-04-05 21:57] crash_overlord: Just play and you'll learn
[2018-04-05 21:58] mqp: i'm pretty sure what polari said "read guides, play, analyse your play" is how you gain it even though you didn't like it when he said it
[2018-04-05 21:58] mqp: that's how everyone i know gains that kind of knowledge in most all games i know
[2018-04-05 21:58] p0lari (Polari): yeah what I'm most puzzled by here is what's really the alternative?
[2018-04-05 21:58] p0lari (Polari): is there a serious competitive game where someone could just tell you how to get good and it was that easy?
[2018-04-05 21:59] mqp: if you find that you are improving or learning slowly then reflect on how you are doing it and fix the bugs in your process? everyone is different, you will have to find a game learning style that works for you
[2018-04-05 21:59] p0lari (Polari): I don't find getting better at prismata to be really any different from getting better at the other games I've played
[2018-04-05 21:59] darkeisbein: I mean there are tons of competetive games where you need to learn the controls first
[2018-04-05 21:59] p0lari (Polari): and no it's not about rote memorisation
[2018-04-05 22:00] darkeisbein: like in fighting games before you can start learning the game you have to learn the real moves
[2018-04-05 22:00] darkeisbein: which is even more stupid
[2018-04-05 22:01] mqp: i personally have 10ks of hours of evidence that for myself, i don't improve at a game unless i play long, maximally serious matches and then analyze them afterwards, nothing else has a noticeable effect
[2018-04-05 22:01] gadget246: i don't want to be told how to get good or for it to be easy
i want to figure things out with logic and analysis, that's why i started playing this game
but i can't follow the logic and analysis that people are using to give me advice
so all the advice is just facts to me that i can memorize or forget about
i want to be able to figure out the things that i'm being told, or at least understand WHY they are the case
[2018-04-05 22:01] gadget246: and i can't currently do that
[2018-04-05 22:01] gadget246: and i can't see a way to gain that
[2018-04-05 22:01] mqp: then start asking specific fundamental questions about things you don't understand, either to yourself or to others, and think about them until you understand the answers
[2018-04-05 22:02] mqp: same way you learn any deep field of inquiry that you don't understand at all yet, you work up from the bottom
[2018-04-05 22:02] .bky_1556 (bky): Ok, the general principle behind wincer > fangs is that you need to be able to both attack and defend. Fang+tarsier+wall requires 18 drones to support, but at least you have the option to drop to fang+husk+wall.
[2018-04-05 22:03] .bky_1556 (bky): At that point you have only a couple of turns to Fang before the Wincer comes in.
[2018-04-05 22:04] .bky_1556 (bky): Then wincer crushes you because you only have a single blastforge for efficient defense-production, while your opponent has BB going.
[2018-04-05 22:04] .bky_1556 (bky): In other words, the wincer gets the first serious attack in before fang does.
[2018-04-05 22:04] .bky_1556 (bky): Also, fang is breach-vulnerable.
[2018-04-05 22:05] awaclus: in general, it just tends to be very difficult to defend against a ton of attack from a low econ position, and wincer is a ton of attack
[2018-04-05 22:05] unctious: Gadget: it sounds like the fundamental questions you weren’t asking were, “how many turns until my opponent is able to do damage to me? Will I be able to get value out of my shadowfangs before he overruns my defenders? Will I have to spend too much money on defenses to make my shadowfangs useful?”
[2018-04-05 22:05] p0lari (Polari): also note that you could have figured this out by calculating a few turns ahead during the game
[2018-04-05 22:06] p0lari (Polari): with a 60s timer you should have time for that
[2018-04-05 22:06] unctious: So those are the questions I would start to ask in future games
[2018-04-05 22:07] gadget246: so it sounds to me like the way that you figured out that wincer would beat fang there was by thinking a bunch of turns ahead. is that accurate? or did you figure it out some other way
[2018-04-05 22:07] mqp: yeah, i evaluate that choice by figuring out how many shadowfangs i need before my second animus is a good investment (i know from experience that the answer is like a small handful) adjusted downward by the presence of husk, and then calculating ahead a few turns to see whether it's plausible that i will be able to buy that many if my opponent does something reasonable
[2018-04-05 22:07] gadget246: thank you unctious that's really helpful
[2018-04-05 22:08] awaclus: also you don't want to mirror your opponent's shadowfang rush half a turn later, because that way they're putting pressure on you earlier, which means you have to defend earlier, which means you won't be putting that much pressure on them, which means they get value from their second animus and you don't

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-04-05) ---
[2018-04-05 22:08] .bky_1556 (bky): Actually, the drone size math is pretty simple. Figure out how much stuff you want to pump each turn when you hit your stride and add up the gold costs.
[2018-04-05 22:09] gadget246: yeah i did actually do that, i just had a plan for "my stride" that lost to my opponent's stride
[2018-04-05 22:10] .bky_1556 (bky): If it's too high vs. any attack response given the defense available, which requires experience to gauge, you need to find a faster line.
[2018-04-05 22:10] .bky_1556 (bky): Which in this case is to make a wincer.
[2018-04-05 22:10] .bky_1556 (bky): Wincer doesn't need to pump stuff regularly, it makes the wincer once.
[2018-04-05 22:12] gadget246: this question might be too broad to have a good answer, but i'll ask it just in case it does have one
is there something I can do as p2 if my opponent is doing the same thing that I was planning on doing but half a turn earlier to beat them, or is it probably too late by that point?
[2018-04-05 22:13] p0lari (Polari): that's not really specific to p2, p2 has the best early conduit and fast animus lines so it's as p1 that this comes up more often
[2018-04-05 22:13] mqp: it can be OK to do it half a turn later, you need to have a plan to take advantage of your half-turn-later of extra economy
[2018-04-05 22:14] mqp: so for example in the early game p2 can play fast animus 1. DD 2. DA and p1 can play natural animus half a turn later 1. DD 2. DD 3. DDA
[2018-04-05 22:14] mqp: generally speaking, p1's line is going to be better when there is some other non-red tech in the set that's useful, like something blue or green

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: And I don't mean it's too weak, I mean you literally can't do that build as P1.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: P2, of course, would rather A+Galv on turn 1.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Try it vs. Pacifist Bot. You'll find you're a gold short on turn 4.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Or alternatively, an E short.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Are you trying to undercut an Elyot Animus or something?

messages.author: strshplyr5046
messages.content: It's an 11RR or BT2 11 Attacker.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: I tried to put that build to a quick masterbot test, forgot the Galvani in the set

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: and found out that Masterbot's terrible at handling T2 Smorcus.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Would have claimed the Rush Master achievement if I didn't already have it.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Rush master is generally harder as P1, though.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Of course, Masterbot doesn't do any better against Galvani variants (JL6Y6-3VpKU or dc6f5-rWjui)

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: So congrats, @StrShPlyr  you've found an exploitable flaw in Master Bot.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: The build is probably hard-countered by DD/DA/1B though. (Edit - this analysis is wrong)

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: You need to choose between clicking smorcus and having defense.
